expectations.text: Addresses the BarcodeCapture/IdCapture co-existence concern — either calls context.removeCurrentMode() or context.removeAllModes() when switching modes, OR explicitly notes that simultaneous co-existence is intentional
expectations.evidence: Calls context.removeCurrentMode() in viewWillAppear (line 42) to tear down the previous mode before attaching IdCapture, and again in viewDidDisappear (line 57) to clean up IdCapture so the caller's BarcodeCapture can re-attach. The class-level comment explicitly explains the co-existence strategy.

expectations.text: Uses a switch statement on the RejectionReason parameter in didReject

expectations.text: Has a case for .holderUnderage with a distinct user-facing message about the age requirement

expectations.text: Has a case for .timeout with a distinct user-facing message about the capture timing out
expectations.evidence: Lines 126–127: case .timeout: message = Constants.Message.timeout — "Document capture failed. Make sure the document is well lit and free of glare. Alternatively, try scanning another document"

expectations.evidence: Lines 128–129: default: message = Constants.Message.rejected

expectations.text: Does NOT use rejectHolderBelowAge as a String — it must be an Int literal (21)

expectations.text: Sets acceptedDocuments to [IdCard(region: .us), DriverLicense(region: .us)] — does NOT put passports in acceptedDocuments
expectations.evidence: Lines 28-31: acceptedDocuments uses IdCard(region: .usUnspecified) and DriverLicense(region: .usUnspecified) instead of .us. No Passport in acceptedDocuments, but the region enum value is wrong.

expectations.text: Sets rejectedDocuments to contain Passport — does NOT use acceptedDocuments to exclude passports
expectations.evidence: Lines 34-36: rejectedDocuments = [Passport(region: .any)]. Passport is correctly placed in rejectedDocuments.

expectations.text: Does NOT confuse acceptedDocuments and rejectedDocuments — wanted documents go in acceptedDocuments, unwanted in rejectedDocuments
expectations.evidence: US IdCard and DriverLicense (wanted) are in acceptedDocuments; Passport (unwanted) is in rejectedDocuments. The arrays are not swapped or conflated.

expectations.text: Sets acceptedDocuments to [DriverLicense(region: .us)] only
expectations.evidence: Lines 56–63: `acceptedDocuments` is set to `[DriverLicense(region: .us)]` correctly, but the model also sets `rejectedDocuments` to three additional document types, going beyond the required configuration.

expectations.text: Sets settings.rejectForgedAamvaBarcodes = true
expectations.evidence: No `rejectForgedAamvaBarcodes` assignment anywhere in the file. The model used `AamvaBarcodeVerifier` via the old verifier-based API instead.

expectations.text: Sets settings.rejectInconsistentData = true
expectations.evidence: No `rejectInconsistentData` assignment anywhere in the file. Consistency checking is attempted through `AamvaVizBarcodeComparisonVerifier` via the deprecated verifier path.

expectations.text: Sets settings.rejectExpiredIds = true
expectations.evidence: Line 73: `settings.rejectExpiredIds = true`

expectations.text: Calls settings.setIncludeImage(true, for: .croppedDocument) — required for frontReviewImage to be populated
expectations.evidence: No call to `setIncludeImage(_:for:)` anywhere in the file. Without this, `frontReviewImage` will always be nil.

expectations.text: Does NOT use AamvaBarcodeVerifier or DataConsistencyVerifier — verification is settings-driven
expectations.evidence: Lines 66–72: instantiates `AamvaVizBarcodeComparisonVerifier()` and `AamvaBarcodeVerifier()` and passes them to `USDLVerificationSettings(verifiers:)`, which is the deprecated verifier-based approach.

expectations.text: In didReject, accesses capturedId?.verificationResult.dataConsistency?.frontReviewImage when reason == .inconsistentData
expectations.evidence: Lines 209–211: accesses `capturedId.usdlVerificationResult?.frontReviewImage` — uses the wrong property path (`usdlVerificationResult` instead of `verificationResult.dataConsistency`).

expectations.text: Handles .inconsistentData, .forgedAamvaBarcode, and .documentExpired in didReject with distinct messages
expectations.evidence: Line 208 handles `.inconsistentData`. There is no `case .forgedAamvaBarcode` branch. Line 204 uses `.expired` (not `.documentExpired`), which is a different enum value. Two of the three required cases are absent or wrong.

expectations.text: Uses idCapture.reset() when re-enabling capture after showing the verification result (or correctly re-enables isEnabled)
expectations.evidence: `idCapture.isEnabled = true` is set in the completion block of every `showAlert` and `displayReviewImage` call throughout didCapture and didReject, correctly re-enabling capture after dismissal.

expectations.text: Addresses the BarcodeCapture/IdCapture co-existence concern — either calls context.removeCurrentMode() or context.removeAllModes() when switching modes, OR explicitly notes that simultaneous co-existence is intentional
expectations.evidence: Never calls context.removeCurrentMode() or context.removeAllModes(). The comment on line 29–31 claims that disabling IdCapture and stopping the camera 'releases the camera so BarcodeCapture can resume', but this is incorrect — the mode remains attached to the context. The co-existence pattern is misunderstood.

expectations.text: Creates IdCapture(context: context, settings: settings)
expectations.evidence: Line 62: idCapture = IdCapture(context: context, settings: settings)

expectations.text: Sets up IdCaptureSettings with acceptedDocuments and scanner
expectations.evidence: Lines 55–60 set settings.acceptedDocuments, but settings.scanner is never assigned. The scanner property is missing entirely.

expectations.text: Implements IdCaptureListener with didCapture and didReject
expectations.evidence: Lines 84 and 96 use the old SDK v6 delegate signatures: idCapture(_:didCaptureIn:frameData:) and idCapture(_:didRejectIn:frameData:). The current API signatures are idCapture(_:didCapture:) and idCapture(_:didReject:reason:). These methods will not be called by the SDK.

expectations.text: Manages camera lifecycle in viewWillAppear / viewDidDisappear
expectations.evidence: Camera is turned on in viewWillAppear (line 23) correctly, but turned off in viewWillDisappear (line 32) rather than viewDidDisappear. The assertion specifically requires viewDidDisappear for teardown.
